hypothesis: Under DAC-relevant conditions (T = 298 K, pCO2 = 0.4 mbar, RH = 50%), branched polyamine sorbents containing >60% secondary amines and hydrophobic backbone spacers will exhibit at least 20% higher equilibrium CO2 uptake (mmol g−1) than linear polyamine analogues with the same overall amine density due to reduced competitive H2O adsorption.
